Content:
KYC HK Activation Flow

Admin Dashboard Workflow

The Admin Dashboard is an interface used by MO Agents to perform the Know Your Customer (KYC) process for merchants onboarding with Xendit. KYC is an essential verification step that helps us confirm the identity, legitimacy, and risk profile of each merchant to meet regulatory requirements and protect our platform.

This section describes the complete Admin Dashboard workflow — from initial registration from the merchant side to how MO processes the merchant’s application.

The Admin Dashboard workflow includes the following steps:

No.

Step

Screen Shot

Who

First KYC

1

  • Go Onboarding Management

  • change country view to Hong Kong

*Ask your manager for regional access

MO

2

Click on start KYC > click Screen Merchant

  • Tick “Digital” or “Non-digital” for Industry Type

  • Follow checks in sections below

    • Legal Documents

    • Business Proof

  • Check for incomplete/wrong/unclear documents

  • Ensure that liveness check has been completed for authorized signatory

  • Click on “Save and Screen”

For cases where MO is unclear on the details submitted by the merchant

  • Legal documents: Escalate to Chun Yu (Cary) Loin #compliance-requests

  • Business Proof: Escalate to Risk in #risk-requests

MO

3

Screening Status and Actions: follow checks in Sanction Screening

  • No Alert–No action needed

  • Negative–No action needed

  • Decision required–Check on ComplyAdvantage

  • Screening failed–Rescreen

  • Others–triggered by MO action on Comply Advantage

MO, Compliance

4

If LDD, BDD, and Screening have passed, MO will proceed to request the additional FinOps requirements via email. MO is responsible for reaching out to the merchant to obtain the required information.

Note:

This will be based on the ticked business intent of the merchant in OM.

MO needs to request FinOps clearance by filling out the sheet here: Regional Sales <> Product SheetPlease only fill the following columns. The MO section of the sheet starts from Row 331.

For exact steps, please refer to the regional onboarding SOP: [SOP] MO Regional Onboarding

Please check from time to time if approved by finops already. Approved entities are highlighted in green.

If merchant needs to be prioritized please raise to #regional-fin-fx-ops and tag Aldo Adhitya

MO/Finops

6

If the merchant is AML HIGH RISK RATING and also include in one of the industries listed here once approved by FinOps, raise a request in #compliance-request for clearance to onboard the merchant. Include the following details:

  • BID

  • Merchant Name

  • Industry

  • Any Notes on the Merchant (Additional License Submitted by Merchant)

  • EDD Documents if there is any

If the merchant is not HIGH RISK, this step can be disregarded

MO/Compliance

6

All global merchants will now require Naz Approval via #gtm-strategic-oppty-review called CRO Approval Workflow

What will happen:

  • On-going Merchant - Still continue with the review, once ready to go live and already have needed clearance, raise request to Naz approval

  • For Merchant under Sales/AM - Sales and AM will be responsible and raising the request

  • For Self-serve - MO can raise for self-serve

FORMAT ON THE WORKFLOW:

  • Request Type: Global Account Go-Live (1Q26 only; while onboarding is blocked)

  • Your Name: Name of the Requestor

  • Customer Name: Name of the Merchant

  • Country of Incorporation: Country where the merchant was incorporated

  • Industry: Industry type of the merchant

  • Use Case:

    • Payment Acceptance

    • Payouts

    • FX/Remittance/Cross-border

    • Platform

    • Identity Verification

    • EDC

    • Other (please specify)

  • Use Case Detail:

    • BDD Assessment: An overview of the merchant’s business and industry.

    • LDD Assessment: An overview of the UBO details, their locations, and confirmation that all required documents are in order (We may request David's help in reviewing documents for unfamiliar countries or if your unsure).

    • EDD: Additional information about the merchant related to its industry, including the EDD questionnaire and any additional licenses.

  • Impact: Input Estimated Monthly Transaction Amount

MO/Sales

Resubmission

1

Situations for resubmission request

  • Additional documents needed

  • Incomplete/obscure documents

  • Incomplete information on official sites, eg. legal name(not business name), address, price, etc

  • Unsure of the nature of business–ask for invoice to customer or explanation

For merchants with Sales/AM: tag Sales/AM for resubmission requirements in #mo-request

For self-serve merchants: submit to merchant directly via “resubmission” flow on Admin Dashboard

MO, CPS, Sales

After Resubmission

1

Check and tick/cross for resubmitted parts, go live if all parts are sufficient

MO

2

For insufficient cases:

  • Approach @compliance-hk for compliance related issues in #compliance-request

  • Ask merchant for next round resubmission if needed

Business Due Diligence

The Business Due Diligence (BDD) process guides MO Agents in verifying a merchant’s business information during onboarding. Agents review the merchant’s website, business description, and industry classification to ensure they align with Xendit’s policies and risk requirements.

Below are the general criteria a merchant's website must meet during the verification process:

  • The website or App must be ready, live, and accessible.

  • The website should match the nature of the business registered with Xendit

  • The website must reflect and match the contact information (such as business email, phone number, business address) registered with Xendit

  • We prefer websites and apps with catalogs or products with pricing details that are available to purchase via checkout flow.

No

Type of Business Proof

What to check

For online merchants

1

Website

  • Accessible

  • Original (no signs of plagiarism)

  • No signs of suspicious activities (e.g. gambling)

  • Does not contain negative reviews indicating fraud

  • Products are clearly present and aligned with the business

  • Pricing is clearly present and realistic

  • If an App, it must be available on official app stores (Tencent App Store (also known as MyApp), Huawei App Market, and Xiaomi App Store)

Additional checks in HK:

  • Ensure website mentioned merchant’s legal name

    • If no:

      • Self-serve merchants: escalate to @compliance-hk in #compliance request

      • Sales/AM/CS merchant: request for email confirmation of merchant name (see template below)

    • If yes: proceed to step 4 in SOP

Email template for name confirmation:

Email subject: Regarding Support of Website of Merchant

Email content:

To HKMPS,

I am writing to confirm that merchant name is operating the name [Merchant legal name submitted to Xendit] on the website [Merchant website URL].

[Director Name]

Director

On behalf of (Merchant Name)

For offline merchants (Refer to the section that applies based on the merchant’s submission)

2

Photos of Offline Presence

One or more of these must be present:

  • Signage with the business name

  • (Preferred) Signage with the legal name

  • (Preferred) Products that are aligned with the business activity

  • (Preferred) Photos match the business pictures that are found online

3

Contract between buyer & merchant

  • Showing the legal name of the business

  • Showing the trade name of the business

  • Detailing transactions of products that are aligned with the nature of the business

  • Convincing visuals (does not look fabricated or poorly/unprofessionally made)

4

Proof of payment

  • Showing the legal name of the business as the recipient bank account

  • Showing the amount of transactions that are aligned with their product pricing

5

Screenshot of communication between merchant and buyer confirming product ordering

  • Detailing transactions of products that are aligned with the nature of the business

  • Convincing visuals (does not look fabricated or poorly/unprofessionally made)

Legal Due Diligence

The Legal Due Diligence (LDD) process enables MO Agents to verify the validity of legal documents submitted by merchants during onboarding. Agents review required documents in the Admin Dashboard and mark them as valid or request resubmission if invalid.

The steps below outline the standard workflow for completing LDD checks:

Steps

Document Sample

What to check

MO

Must be collected

1

Certificate of Incorporation

Merchant Submitted Copy

  • Ensure it is submitted on Admin Dashboard

  • Ensure legal name matches what has been submitted on Admin Dashboard

2

Purchased Copy Verification

  • Log in to HK CR registry

    • e-Service Portal > Registered User Services > Search

    • Enter exact legal name based on merchant submitted certificate

  • Screenshot from CR registry check

  • Verify screenshot with Certificate of Incorporation

    • Full Legal Name

    • Live Status

    • Ensure screenshot includes “System Clock” - time of screenshot

  • Upload CR Registry check onto Admin Dashboard under “business document”

3

Business Registration Certificate

Merchant Submitted Copy

  • Ensure it is submitted on Admin Dashboard

  • Ensure expiry date is valid

  • Ensure legal name matches CI

4

Purchase copy only if merchant is a “sole proprietorship” or “partnership”

  • Log in to BR registry

    • Search now > Begin application > Apply for extract of information > Enter full BRN (as shown in BRC) > Electronic extract

    • Purchase BRC

  • Ensure purchased copy matches what is submitted by merchant

    • Partner name

    • Sole proprietor name

  • Upload purchased document on Admin Dashboard under “business document”

3

NAR1/NNC1 - Annual Report

Merchant Submitted Copy

  • Ensure it is submitted on Admin Dashboard

  • Ensure legal name matches

Purchasing NAR 1/NNC 1 requirements

  • Purchase latest NAR 1: if company has been incorporated > 1 year

  • Purchase NNC1: if company has no NAR 1 available in registry (i.e. when company has been incorporated < 1 year)

Purchased copy

  • Log in to HK CR registry

    • e-Service Portal > Unregistered User Services (sign up with all capabilities) > Search

    • Enter exact legal name based on merchant submitted certificate

    • Document Index > Financial Report (if no NAR1, select NNC1)

    • Purchase NAR 1

  • Ensure Purchased copy matches what is submitted by merchant

  • Verify NAR 1/NNC1

    • Directors name

    • Shareholding structure & UBOs (owning more than 25% of company)

      • No corporate shareholders

  • UBOs: require submission of ID - proceed to step 4

  • Authorised Signatory: declared Authorised Signatory is a Director of the company - require submission of ID - proceed to step 4

  • Upload purchased document on Admin Dashboard under “business document”

CR Account Login:

  • Username: HKMPSCR

  • Password: G5k49kog6388

Conditional Steps

  • If there are shareholders who are not individuals: check step 3a.

  • If authorized signatory is not a director: check to step 3b.

4

Option 1: Scanned HKID/CN Copy

Proof of Address

UBO, Authorized Signatory

  • Ensure it is submitted on Admin Dashboard

Option 1: Scanned HKID Copy

Proof of address must be dated within 6 months and can be any of

  • Utility bill with address

  • Tenancy agreement

  • Bank Account Statement

Option 2: Scanned Passport

UBO, Authorized Signatory

  • Ensure it is submitted on Admin Dashboard

Option 2: Scanned Passport

  • Must be the correct format

  • Name must match authorized person’s name

  • Must have an address, otherwise, MO must ask for proof of address

Proof of address must be dated within 6 months and can be any of

  • Utility bill with address

  • Tenancy agreement

  • Bank Account Statement

Conditional Requirements

3a

Shareholders identified in NAR1/NNC1: Not Individuals

Require a shareholding chart and identification of individual UBOs (owning more than 25% of company)

Share Xendit - Shareholding Chart Template_06NOV2024

  • If merchant has Sales/AM: tag Sales/AM

  • If merchant is self-serve: Request resubmission

3b

Authorized Signatory not identified as a Director in NAR1/NNC1

Require board resolution from company directors to grant authorized signatory power

Share XPS - Board Resolution (For Companies) Template_06NOV2024

  • If merchant has Sales/AM: tag Sales/AM

  • If merchant is self-serve: Request resubmission

FAQ

What happens if merchant has inputted the wrong “business entity type” (e.g. they have put in “sole proprietorship” when their BRC states they are a “corporation”

Raise to #product-issues
